explicit_text: I was born in Pasadena almost sixty years ago and have lived there off and on most of my life. I attended several schools in Pasadena, including Luther Burbank Grammar School and University School for high school. Even now, Pasadena remains my home, and it has been a central place throughout my life.
implicit_text: I was born in the city known for its rose parade and historic landmarks almost sixty years ago and have lived there off and on most of my life. I attended several schools in that city, including Luther Burbank Grammar School and University School for high school. Even now, this city remains my home, and it has been a central place throughout my life.

explicit_text: After living in Pasadena for a while, my family moved to Altadena where we stayed for about twenty years. I went to Charles W. Elliot Junior High School in Altadena. It was an important part of my youth and schooling before I returned to Pasadena for high school.
implicit_text: After living in the city famous for its annual parade for a while, my family moved to the nearby community nestled in the foothills where we stayed for about twenty years. I went to Charles W. Elliot Junior High School in that foothill community. It was an important part of my youth and schooling before I returned to the original city for high school.
cues: Charles W. Elliot Junior High School

explicit_text: I attended Woodbury College right around the end of World War II, when it was filled with GI students. It was located on Wilshire Boulevard near Figueroa Street at the time. The commercial art department wasn’t very strong then, but it was a good place for me to start since I was very young, not yet twenty years old.
implicit_text: I attended a small college known for its commercial art program right around the end of World War II, when it was filled with GI students. It was located on a major boulevard near a well-known street in Los Angeles at the time. The commercial art department wasn’t very strong then, but it was a good place for me to start since I was very young, not yet twenty years old.

explicit_text: Woodbury College was located on Wilshire Boulevard near Figueroa Street when I attended it. This area was where I began my formal art education in commercial art. I’m not sure if the college is still there now, but that location was significant during my early college years.
implicit_text: That small college was located on a major boulevard near a famous street when I attended it. This area was where I began my formal art education in commercial art. I’m not sure if the college is still there now, but that location was significant during my early college years.

explicit_text: Figueroa Street was close to where Woodbury College was situated on Wilshire Boulevard when I studied there after the war. This intersection was part of the urban landscape of Los Angeles that I experienced during my early art education. It’s a detail I remember because it marked the place where I started to take art seriously.
implicit_text: The well-known street was close to where the small college was situated on the major boulevard when I studied there after the war. This intersection was part of the urban landscape of Los Angeles that I experienced during my early art education. It’s a detail I remember because it marked the place where I started to take art seriously.

explicit_text: After Woodbury, I went to Art Center College of Design for a semester. At that time, it was located way out on Third Street in what used to be an old girls’ school. Unfortunately, I was drafted during that semester, so I didn’t get to complete my studies there.
implicit_text: After that initial college, I went to a prestigious design school for a semester. At that time, it was located way out on Third Street in what used to be an old girls’ school. Unfortunately, I was drafted during that semester, so I didn’t get to complete my studies there.

explicit_text: After the Korean War, I attended Chouinard Art Institute, which I consider the best school I went to. I was there when the school was at its peak, with Mrs. Chouinard and the teachers still in their prime. It was a great experience, and I feel lucky to have been part of that environment even though I didn’t fully appreciate it at the time.
implicit_text: After the Korean War, I attended a renowned art institute, which I consider the best school I went to. I was there when the school was at its peak, with the founder and the teachers still in their prime. It was a great experience, and I feel lucky to have been part of that environment even though I didn’t fully appreciate it at the time.

explicit_text: One of my most influential teachers at Chouinard was Don Graham, who taught drawing and composition. He had a unique approach, pushing students to tackle difficult aspects of art rather than just copying. Don Graham also taught part-time at Disney Studios in the early 1930s to improve their artists’ drawing skills for Snow White, which I found fascinating.
implicit_text: One of my most influential teachers at that art institute was a drawing and composition instructor who had a unique approach, pushing students to tackle difficult aspects of art rather than just copying. He also taught part-time at a major animation studio in the early 1930s to improve their artists’ drawing skills for a landmark animated film, which I found fascinating.

explicit_text: William Moore was another great teacher I had at Chouinard; he taught color and design. He was probably an interior designer by profession and had a unique system influenced by cubism, though he never used that term. His method of painting colors on paper and cutting them out to create designs was very instructional and helped me a lot, especially for book layout work.
implicit_text: Another great teacher I had at that art institute taught color and design. He was probably an interior designer by profession and had a unique system influenced by cubism, though he never used that term. His method of painting colors on paper and cutting them out to create designs was very instructional and helped me a lot, especially for book layout work.

explicit_text: Walt Disney was the visionary who recognized that his animators needed better drawing skills to create Snow White. He hired Don Graham from Chouinard in the early 1930s to teach his artists. Although I never met Disney personally, his decision had a big impact on the quality of animation and the art education connected to Disney Studios.
implicit_text: The visionary behind the famous animation studio recognized that his animators needed better drawing skills to create the first full-length animated feature. He hired the drawing and composition instructor from that art institute in the early 1930s to teach his artists. Although I never met the visionary personally, his decision had a big impact on the quality of animation and the art education connected to that studio.

entity: Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs
explicit_text: Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs was a groundbreaking project for Disney in the early 1930s. Walt Disney felt his artists weren’t skilled enough in drawing to produce it, so he brought in Don Graham to train them. This film marked a turning point where art education and animation merged in a new way.
implicit_text: The groundbreaking animated film from the early 1930s was a major project for the famous animation studio. The studio head felt his artists weren’t skilled enough in drawing to produce it, so he brought in the art instructor to train them. This film marked a turning point where art education and animation merged in a new way.

explicit_text: I started working at Disney Studios in 1955, well after Don Graham had taught there part-time in the early 1930s. When I arrived, Don Graham was working part-time on his book, The Art of Animation, in a room at the studio. Disney Studios was a place where art and animation came together, and it was fascinating to be part of that legacy.
implicit_text: I started working at the famous animation studio in 1955, well after the drawing instructor had taught there part-time in the early 1930s. When I arrived, he was working part-time on his book in a room at the studio. That studio was a place where art and animation came together, and it was fascinating to be part of that legacy.

explicit_text: Don Graham was writing The Art of Animation around the time I joined Disney Studios in 1955. The book was published a few years later, around 1957 or 1958, and he worked on it part-time at the studio. It was an important work that reflected his deep knowledge of art and animation.
implicit_text: The drawing instructor was writing a significant book about animation around the time I joined the famous animation studio in 1955. The book was published a few years later, around 1957 or 1958, and he worked on it part-time at the studio. It was an important work that reflected his deep knowledge of art and animation.

explicit_text: I was drafted during the Korean War, which interrupted my studies at Art Center College of Design. After my service, I resumed my education at Chouinard Art Institute. The war was a significant event that shaped the timing of my art education and career path.
